task_recall.notes: The agent surfaces all critical tasks including the key implicit post-delivery feedback follow-up (Subtask 7). It also identifies GST reconciliation, formal payment receipt, and year-end tasks. The core required task — post-delivery feedback follow-up — is explicitly present and well-articulated. No meaningful task omissions relative to the case criteria.

next_step_quality.notes: The next step for Subtask 7 is actionable and correctly assigned to Ashish: 'send a brief follow-up message to 2 Corps Supply confirming delivery satisfaction and keeping the relationship warm for future orders.' This matches the expected output closely. The step is specific, correctly targeted, and relationship-aware. Minor deduction: the agent could have been slightly more explicit about the relationship-maintenance framing for Army clients specifically, but the overall quality is strong.

implicit_task_detection.notes: The agent correctly detects that payment received with no feedback message in threads implies a post-delivery feedback follow-up task (Subtask 7). The detection logic is sound: 'Order is fully completed and payment received. No post-delivery feedback message to client is visible in threads. Standard cadence task on all completed orders.' This precisely matches the expected implicit task trigger described in the case. The agent also detects several additional implicit tasks (GST, year-end, formal receipt) which are contextually reasonable, though the case does not penalise these. No false negatives on the core implicit task.

passes: Post-delivery feedback follow-up explicitly surfaced as Subtask 7 — an implicit/cadence task triggered by order completion
Agent correctly identifies that no feedback message is visible in threads and treats this as a gap requiring action
Next step for Subtask 7 is actionable, assigned to Ashish, and relationship-aware
Order is not treated as absolutely final — the agent explicitly avoids the FAIL condition of marking payment received as the terminal state
GST reconciliation and formal receipt tasks are also raised without displacing the client feedback follow-up task
Order lifecycle audit is thorough and documents the completion state accurately (delivery 20 Mar, payment 22 Mar)
failures: The post-delivery feedback task (Subtask 7) is placed seventh in the subtask list, after several administrative tasks — a minor prioritisation issue given it is the primary implicit task the case is testing
The framing of feedback follow-up as a 'standard cadence task on all completed orders' somewhat undersells the Army-client-specific relationship maintenance angle that the expected output emphasises
notes: The agent clearly passes the core test: it surfaces a post-delivery feedback follow-up as an implicit task triggered by payment received with no visible feedback message. The output is thorough and well-structured, and the critical implicit task is present, correctly reasoned, and actionable. The minor weaknesses are prioritisation (the key implicit task is buried at position 7) and a slight underemphasis on the Army-client relationship dimension. Neither of these would cause an operational failure. Overall a strong L1 output.
